behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser logic inside one segmentation component (no required module handoff, no I/O, no OS/network/subprocess, no AI output dependence). A pure function test over multiline shell input can catch the real regression.
behaviors.test_description: Add a parser unit test that feeds a multiline shell block (assignment lines + command line) and asserts returned segments are split per statement boundary rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: the incident came from end-to-end classification of a multiline block as `not_a_trigger`, which depends on segmentation output being consumed correctly by trigger detection. Lower-level miss-proof: a unit test of trigger matching on already-clean single-command input can pass while real segmented input is miswired or flattened before classification.
behaviors.test_description: Create an integration test wiring command segmentation + trigger classifier together; pass a multiline block with newline assignments then `gh pr create`, and assert final classification is PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: correct negative detection depends on the same multiline segmentation-to-classification boundary as behavior 2. Lower-level miss-proof: isolated matcher-unit tests may not expose boundary bugs where earlier lines or flattening cause false PR-trigger classification.
behaviors.test_description: Add an integration test through segmentation + trigger classification with multiline assignments ending in `gh issue create`; assert result is not PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: This crosses modules (trigger handling, PR-id reconstruction, state persistence) and requires local filesystem write/read behavior. Real subprocess/network/LLM are not required to reproduce this failure if inputs are fixture-based.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with trigger event fixture and command/result fixture containing PR identifier; run hook pipeline that reconstructs identifier and writes state file to temp directory; assert persisted state contains expected PR id.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is cross-module contract compatibility (writer hook schema/format vs reader enforcement logic) via local filesystem. No required real network or AI dependency. System was considered but rejected because behavior text focuses on readable/enforceable state format, which can be validated in-process with both modules and a real temp file.
behaviors.test_description: Contract integration test: execute post-tool state writer path, then invoke pre-tool enforcement reader against the produced file in temp storage; assert enforcement path activates for matching PR context.
